inclusion_criteria: Adults with Persistent Spinal Pain Syndrome Type 2 (PSPS-T2), i.e., chronic spinal pain persisting after prior spinal surgery (including studies using terms such as failed back surgery syndrome), as defined by the study.

Randomized or quasi-randomized controlled trials, or prospective comparative studies, evaluating neuromodulation (e.g., spinal cord stimulation), minimally invasive treatments (e.g., epidural injections), conservative treatments (physiotherapy, cognitive/behavioral training, medication management), or reoperation.
Comparators include placebo/sham, conservative treatment, or head-to-head comparisons between eligible modalities.
Report pain intensity outcomes using validated scales (e.g., VAS, NRS) with sufficient data to calculate standardized mean differences; full-text, peer-reviewed human studies.
exclusion_criteria: Non-comparative or ineligible designs: single-arm studies, case series/reports, retrospective studies without a control group, cross-sectional or qualitative studies, reviews, editorials, conference abstracts only, protocols, animal or in vitro studies.
Wrong population: no prior spinal surgery, primary spinal pain without surgery, acute postoperative pain, non-spinal pain conditions, or pediatric populations.

Wrong intervention/comparator: interventions outside neuromodulation, minimally invasive injections, conservative care, or reoperation; or comparisons that do not involve placebo/sham, conservative care, or head-to-head among eligible modalities.
Do not report pain intensity outcomes or provide insufficient data to estimate effect sizes.
screening_criteria.description: Eligibility criteria to select comparative studies assessing neuromodulation, minimally invasive injections, conservative care, or reoperation for adults with PSPS-T2, requiring pain intensity outcomes suitable for standardized mean difference estimation.
